findings.title: n8n Ni8mare Unauthenticated RCE Chain
findings.summary: Unauthenticated arbitrary file read via Content-Type confusion chained with expression injection sandbox bypass for full RCE. Public automated exploit available.

findings.title: GNU inetutils telnetd Unauthenticated RCE
findings.summary: 11-year-old bug in telnetd allows remote root shell via malformed USER environment variable. SafeBreach Labs PoC published.

findings.title: osTicket Arbitrary File Read + CNEXT RCE
findings.summary: PHP filter abuse via mPDF library for arbitrary file exfiltration, chainable to RCE via CVE-2024-2961 CNEXT. Full exploit toolkit from Horizon3.ai.

findings.title: AdonisJS Bodyparser Path Traversal
findings.summary: Critical path traversal allowing arbitrary file writing on server via bodyparser.

findings.title: node-tar Arbitrary File Overwrite
findings.summary: Arbitrary file overwrite via crafted tar archive in node-tar npm package.

findings.title: ShinyHunters SSO Vishing Campaign
findings.summary: ShinyHunters targeting 100+ orgs via voice phishing of SSO accounts. Latest confirmed victim: Optimizely (Feb 11). Data exfiltration from connected SaaS apps followed by extortion.

findings.title: Phoenix Contact TC Router Command Injection
findings.summary: Authenticated command injection in industrial TC Router series.

findings.title: Native Instruments Native Access macOS LPE (UNPATCHED)
findings.summary: Local privilege escalation via DYLIB injection in privileged helper. Vendor unresponsive, no patch available.

findings.title: February Linux Patch Wednesday — 632 CVEs
findings.summary: 632 vulnerabilities patched including 305 in kernel. 2 confirmed ITW (CVE-2026-2441, CVE-2025-14847). 56 with public exploits including PyTorch RCE, Roundcube SFB, pgAdmin RCE, Grafana EoP.

findings.title: Android Mental Health Apps Security Flaws
findings.summary: 1,575 vulnerabilities found across 10 mental health apps. 54 high-severity issues. Exposed therapy transcripts, mood logs, and medication data.

findings.title: Microsoft Office RCE (Trending)
findings.summary: Remote code execution in Microsoft Office — flagged as trending vulnerability by Positive Technologies.

findings.title: Desktop Window Manager Info Disclosure (Trending)
findings.summary: Information disclosure in Desktop Window Manager — trending vulnerability.

noted.summary: Chrome zero-day — confirmed ITW exploitation in Linux Patch Wednesday context
BeyondTrust RS/PRA RCE — detailed Rapid7 technical analysis now on AttackerKB, same endpoint as CVE-2024-12356
MongoDB MongoBleed — confirmed ITW exploitation in Linux context
OpenSSL RCE — now confirmed with Linux public exploit
ShinyHunters SSO vishing campaign expanding — Optimizely is latest confirmed victim (Feb 11)
